headline: SK Hynix Surges on Nasdaq Debut; Iran Ceasefire Ends, Oil Jumps
summary: SK Hynix ADRs surged over 13% on their Nasdaq debut, signaling strong investor confidence in AI-driven memory demand. Geopolitical tensions escalated as the Iran ceasefire ended, driving oil prices higher amid concerns over Strait of Hormuz transit risks. Investors are also preparing for a packed week of earnings and CPI data.

bullets.lead: SK Hynix ADRs debuted strongly on Nasdaq.

segments.text: The company's American Depositary Receipts surged 12.8% to 13.1% on their first day of trading, raising $26.5 billion in the largest-ever first-time share sale in the US by a foreign company.

sources.title: SK하이닉스, 나스닥 입성 첫날 13.1%↑…나스닥 사장 "블록버스터급"

segments.text: Intel's 14A node is reportedly slipping beyond 2030, while Samsung's 4nm capacity is filling up due to high HBM4 demand.

sources.title: Insight: TSMC's 2nm lead widens, Intel 14A slips beyond 2030, Samsung 4nm fills on HBM4 demand

bullets.lead: Aehr received a follow-on production order for silicon photonics testing.

segments.text: This order from its lead customer indicates a volume ramp for automated, high-volume burn-in systems.

bullets.lead: Inflation concerns are rising due to tariffs, the Iran war, and AI buildout.

segments.text: A Fed report cited 'stepped-up' inflation from these factors.

sources.title: Fed report cites 'stepped-up' inflation due to tariffs, Iran war, AI buildout

bullets.lead: Hyperscaler AI capex payoff risks are a growing concern.

segments.text: If the return on AI capital expenditure takes longer than anticipated, it could lead to disappointing cash flows and earnings for hyperscalers, potentially triggering a Mag 7 sell-off.

bullets.lead: Investors face a packed week of earnings and CPI data.

segments.text: This week's schedule includes significant corporate reports and key inflation figures, alongside ongoing geopolitical headlines from Iran.

sources.title: Wall St Week Ahead Investors to grapple with packed week of earnings, CPI, Iran headlines

bullets.lead: The Iran ceasefire has ended, with talks on a deal to continue.

segments.text: President Trump stated the ceasefire was over, leading to US strikes on Iranian targets and increased oil prices due to Strait of Hormuz transit risks.

sources.title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over But Talks on Deal to Continue

bullets.lead: Oil prices jumped following renewed Middle East skirmishes.

segments.text: Occidental's quarterly realized oil prices rose, and US pain at the pump worsened as hostilities derailed shipping and lifted crude prices.

sources.title: Occidental’s quarterly realized oil prices jump amid Iran war disruption
US pain at the pump worsens after more US-Iran fighting lifts oil prices
Oil Prices Wobble As a Week of Hostilities Derails Shipping
Oil Posts Weekly Gains on Renewed Middle East Skirmishes
Oil Tankers Still Cross Hormuz on Oman Side Despite Flare Up

bullets.lead: Volkswagen faces production cuts and plunging sales in China.

segments.text: The German automaker's troubles in the region are leading to reduced output.

sources.title: How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China

bullets.lead: Susquehanna is stepping back from China venture deals.

segments.text: An early backer of ByteDance, the firm is reportedly reducing its involvement in China-focused venture capital.

sources.title: Susquehanna, an Early Backer of ByteDance, Is Stepping Back From China Venture Deals

bullets.lead: Nvidia rallied on a guidance beat.

segments.text: The company's strong guidance contributed to positive market sentiment.

sources.title: Volkswagen Is Cutting Production as Sales in China Plunge

bullets.lead: Several Asian tech firms posted record revenues.

segments.text: ASE Holdings reported record 2Q26 revenue, investing $40M in South Korea for AI packaging, while Sigurd and Acer unit Highpoint also saw record quarters driven by AI servers and Asia-Pacific growth.

sources.title: ASE Holdings posts record 2Q26 revenue, bets US$40M on South Korea as AI packaging demand surges

bullets.lead: Wall Street ended higher as investors turned to earnings season.

segments.text: The market showed strength as the focus shifted to corporate results.

sources.title: Wall Street ends higher as investors turn to earnings season

bullets.lead: Meta plans to double its AI infrastructure to 14GW by 2027.

segments.text: This expansion includes a new $10 billion data center in Canada, potentially necessitating an equity raise.

bullets.lead: Meta's Muse Image Tool helped its stock achieve its best week in years.

segments.text: The positive market reaction followed advancements in its AI image generation capabilities.

sources.title: Meta’s Muse Image Tool Boost Helps Stock Hit Best Week In Years

bullets.lead: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6, repositioning ChatGPT as a persistent agent layer.

segments.text: This shift aims for autonomous, recurring workloads, which is structurally positive for AI infrastructure.

bullets.lead: xAI's Grok 4.5 has reportedly achieved Anthropic Opus-level performance.

segments.text: This positions Grok strongly in the coding-agent market, bringing xAI back into the frontier AI model race.

bullets.lead: The memory industry is shifting towards custom silicon and HBM.

segments.text: Architectures like Qualcomm's HBC are reshaping revenue structures and valuations, moving away from commodity hardware.

bullets.lead: Micron plans to invest over $250 billion in US manufacturing through 2035.

segments.text: This significant investment contributes to increased CapEx guidance for the current fiscal year and beyond.

x_themes.topic: xAI's Grok 4.5 re-enters frontier AI model race
x_themes.summary: xAI's Grok 4.5 has reportedly achieved Anthropic Opus-level performance and cost efficiency, positioning it strongly in the coding-agent market and bringing xAI back into the frontier AI model race.

x_themes.topic: Meta's planned 14GW AI compute infrastructure expansion by 2027
x_themes.summary: Meta reportedly aims to double its AI infrastructure to 14GW of compute deployed by 2027, including a new $10 billion datacenter in Canada, potentially necessitating an equity raise.

x_themes.topic: Aehr receives follow-on production order for silicon photonics testing
x_themes.summary: Aehr has received another follow-on production order from its lead silicon photonics customer for a multi-wafer burn-in system, indicating a volume ramp for automated, high-volume production.

x_themes.topic: Photonics oversupply risk and supply/demand balance
x_themes.summary: Concerns are rising about potential oversupply in the photonics sector, particularly for lasers and transceivers, despite massive capacity ramps and constrained supply environments.

x_themes.topic: Memory industry shift to custom silicon/HBM and memory-on-logic
x_themes.summary: The memory industry is evolving from commodity hardware to custom silicon, with HBM and memory-on-logic architectures like Qualcomm's HBC reshaping revenue structures and valuations.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix record-setting US ADR listing
x_themes.summary: SK Hynix Inc. priced its US listing of American depositary receipts at $149 each, raising $26.5 billion in the largest ever first-time share sale in the US by a foreign company, to fund AI computing investments.
Micron plans to invest over $250 billion in US manufacturing through 2035, contributing to increased CapEx guidance for this fiscal year and beyond.

x_themes.topic: US-Iran conflict escalation and oil price impact
x_themes.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire collapsed, leading to US strikes on Iranian targets and a rise in Brent and WTI crude oil prices due to Strait of Hormuz transit risks.

x_themes.topic: Hawkish Fed stance and interest rate outlook
x_themes.summary: The Fed maintained rates but with a hawkish undertone, as FOMC minutes show a split committee with some officials projecting a 2026 hike, impacting high-beta growth stocks.
The market is experiencing a valuation correction and risk-off environment, with AI positioning unwinds and deleveraging causing a sell-off in semis and a rotation into energy and defensives.

x_themes.topic: Hyperscaler AI capex payoff risk and broader market impact
x_themes.summary: Concerns are rising that if AI capex payoff takes longer than expected, hyperscaler cash flows and earnings could disappoint, potentially leading to a Mag 7 sell-off and broader market correction.

x_themes.topic: OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch and agentic AI shift
x_themes.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6, repositioning ChatGPT into a persistent agent layer for autonomous, recurring workloads, structurally positive for AI infrastructure.
ASML is raising its EUV shipment forecasts for 2026/27/28 as supply bottlenecks ease, driven by strong demand from memory and logic.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Q2/Q3 gross margin expected to beat expectations
x_themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 and Q3 gross margins are expected to beat market expectations, driven by a favorable product mix and high volume of rush orders.
